requirements.summary: Rebuild and redeploy the current application as a new version, preserving all existing functionality including the DuckDuckGo search interface, in-app browser modal, error handling, and Google Search Console verification file
acceptanceCriteria: A new version is successfully built and deployed
All existing search functionality remains intact
The in-app browser modal continues to work as before
Error states and loading states display correctly
The Google Search Console verification file remains accessible

file_operations.description: Review and preserve the root component structure that renders SearchInterface inside a full-height flex container, ensuring all existing functionality remains intact

Review and preserve the main search UI component with search form, loading/error/empty states, flattened DuckDuckGo RelatedTopics rendering, in-app browser integration, and context-aware error icons and titles
Review and preserve the fullscreen modal iframe browser with header bar, loading spinner, and fallback UI for sites that block iframe embedding
Review and preserve the search hook that calls the ICP actor, manages loading/error/result state, and includes detailed error parsing for ICP-specific error codes and canister trap messages
Review and preserve the custom hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor using React Query, recreating it when Internet Identity changes
Review and preserve the Internet Identity authentication context provider and hook, managing login/logout state, AuthClient lifecycle, and identity persistence
Review and preserve the utility functions to detect URLs in text and render them as clickable elements, plus URL validation helper
Review and preserve the global CSS with Tailwind directives and OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for light and dark theme color tokens
Review and preserve the Tailwind configuration extending the default theme with OKLCH-based color tokens, custom border radii, shadows, fonts, and accordion keyframe animations
Review and preserve the React app entry point that mounts the root component wrapped with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider
Review and preserve the minimal HTML entry point that mounts the React app into a root div and loads the main TypeScript module
Ensure the Google Search Console site verification file remains accessible at its current location and with its current content
